conditions: Post-accident orthopedic injuries (pelvic fracture and left wrist fracture, healing)
Chronic pain related to injury recovery
medications: Nitroglycerin 0.4 mg sublingual as needed for chest pain
health.mental_health: Since the accident she has intermittent anxiety about falling again and occasional low mood tied to reduced independence; she is doing short-term counseling through a telehealth provider and practices breathing exercises recommended by her cardiac rehab nurse.
recent_visits: Physical therapy twice weekly at Tampa General Hospital outpatient rehab (gait training, balance work, and wrist mobility)
Cardiology follow-up at USF Health Heart Institute (medication review and blood pressure management)
Orthopedic follow-up imaging for fracture healing
Primary care visit for hypertension monitoring and fall-risk evaluation
Home health nurse visit for medication reconciliation after discharge

finance.debt: Carrying about $6,800 on a credit card from post-accident costs (medical copays, mobility aids, and ride services) and is on a hospital payment plan for remaining therapy-related bills not fully covered by Medicare supplement.
recent_transactions: Paid $185 for a rolling walker and shower chair from a medical supply store
Monthly premium payment for Medicare supplement and Part D plan
Co-payments for outpatient physical therapy sessions
Uber/Lyft charges for rides to campus and rehab when she cannot drive
Pharmacy refill for blood pressure and cholesterol medications

relationships: Adult daughter, Aïssatou Diallo, who coordinates rides to therapy and helps with groceries
Neighbor and friend, Marlene Watkins, who checks in after PT days and brings meals occasionally
Former colleague and close friend, Dr. Steven Park, who shares lecture notes and covers a class when needed
Church community at St. Paul AME in Tampa that provides occasional home visits
Physical therapy group acquaintances from outpatient rehab (informal support network)
social.social_media_activity: Light Facebook use (mostly family photos and community events); she avoids posting about her medical details but has mentioned being 'on the mend' after an accident and shares articles about heart health and aging safely at home.
private_conversations: Discussing fear of losing independence and whether she should stop driving temporarily
Talking with her daughter about updating a health care proxy and emergency contacts
Confiding in a friend about pain levels and frustration with slow recovery
Discussing financial stress from copays and reduced teaching load

schedule.work_hours: Teaches two seminar sections (Tues/Thu late morning) and holds virtual office hours on Wednesdays; grading and prep are spread across afternoons with frequent breaks due to fatigue.
regular_appointments: Physical therapy: Mondays and Thursdays at 9:00 AM
Home exercise program: daily 30–40 minutes (split into two sessions)
Blood pressure checks: every morning before breakfast
Cardiology check-in: every 3 months (next one already scheduled)
Counseling session (telehealth): every other Friday at 2:00 PM
upcoming_events: Follow-up orthopedic appointment for repeat imaging next Tuesday at 3:15 PM
Department meeting via Zoom next Wednesday at 11:00 AM
Guest lecture she is scheduled to deliver (virtual) in two weeks
Driver’s evaluation appointment through rehab services later this month
schedule.daily_routine: Wakes around 6:30 AM, takes medications, checks blood pressure, and does gentle stretching; mid-morning is reserved for PT or lecture prep; afternoons are for grading and rest periods; evenings include a short walk with a cane (as tolerated), meal prep with heart-healthy focus, and setting out medications for the next day.

professional.company: Public university in the Tampa Bay area (teaches part-time in the humanities/general education program)
projects: Updating two course shells in the LMS to be fully accessible (captions, readable PDFs, flexible deadlines)
Developing a new lecture module on ethics and public health communication
Writing a short conference proposal with a colleague about community-based learning in retirement-age populations
professional.performance_issues: Since the accident she has requested temporary accommodations (more remote instruction and extended grading timelines). She has missed one in-person session due to a PT flare-up and is concerned about contract renewal if her mobility limitations continue into next term.
workplace_relationships: Supportive department coordinator who helps with room changes closer to elevators
A colleague who informally covers attendance when she has medical appointments
Occasional tension with an administrator about last-minute modality changes (in-person vs. Zoom)

hobbies: Watercolor painting (now done seated due to balance concerns)
Attending small church and community lectures when transportation allows
Gardening on her patio (container herbs and flowers)
Listening to jazz and West African music
habits: Uses a cane indoors and a walker on longer outings while rebuilding strength
Keeps a written medication and blood pressure log for her cardiologist
Limits caffeine and avoids NSAIDs due to heart history and blood pressure
Occasionally uses a medical alert pendant when home alone
Takes short naps after PT sessions due to fatigue
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in a second-floor apartment in Tampa; after the accident her daughter stays over on PT nights when possible, and she has arranged for grocery delivery and a temporary weekly housekeeping service to reduce fall risk.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Heart-healthy, lower-sodium diet (DASH-style): lots of vegetables, fish or lean poultry, whole grains; avoids processed foods and salty snacks; tries to keep portions consistent and drinks mostly water and herbal tea.
